mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive 20d returns occur in more than 55% of triggered instances over a 5-year out-of-sample period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and business demand expansion, generating positive quarterly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Joint positive changes in RSAFS and HOUST yield negative median 60d returns over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during economic recovery phases reflects improving corporate output and capacity utilization, leading to positive short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during recovery phases correlate with negative 5d returns in more than 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding real GDP growth signals strengthening economic fundamentals and higher corporate earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth rates show zero or negative correlation with 60d forward returns over rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and causing negative S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL MoM spikes above 0.4% are followed by positive 20d returns in more than 50% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with falling unemployment rates confirm robust labor market tightening and wage growth, driving positive monthly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence signal yields negative median 20d returns over a 3-year out-of-sample period.

mechanism_narrative: Declining housing starts during late-cycle expansions signal weakening real estate investment and broader demand contraction, resulting in negative short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: HOUST declines in late-cycle phases are followed by positive 5d returns in more than 65% of observations.
